data.title: RED: Write failing tests for LINK event handler in process_outbound
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Write failing unit tests for the LINK event handler that will be added to process_outbound() in bridge-outbound.py. These tests cover: Jira key resolution for both source and target tickets, name-based link type validation with caching, pre-create dedup via existing link query, and graceful degradation on Jira rejection.

## Test Approach

Test 1 — Happy path LINK processing:
Given: A LINK event with event_type="LINK", ticket_id=<source>, data={target_id: <target>, relation: "relates_to"}, and both source and target tickets have SYNC files with jira_keys
When: process_outbound() is called with mock acli_client
Then: acli_client.get_issue_link_types() is called (returns [{"name": "Relates", "id": "10003"}]), acli_client.get_issue_links() is called for dedup (returns []), and acli_client.set_relationship(source_jira_key, target_jira_key, "Relates") is called

Test 2 — Link type caching:
Given: Two LINK events in the same sync batch
When: process_outbound() processes both
Then: get_issue_link_types() is called exactly once (cached after first call)

Test 3 — Pre-create dedup:
Given: A LINK event where get_issue_links() returns an existing "Relates" link between the same source and target
When: process_outbound() is called
Then: set_relationship() is NOT called (link already exists)

Test 4 — Jira rejection (graceful degradation):
Given: A LINK event where acli_client.set_relationship() raises subprocess.CalledProcessError
When: process_outbound() is called
Then: bridge_alert is written to the ticket dir, and processing continues (no exception raised, other events still processed)

Test 5 — Missing target SYNC:
Given: A LINK event where the target ticket has no SYNC file (not yet synced to Jira)
When: process_outbound() is called
Then: bridge_alert is written, link creation is skipped, processing continues

Test 6 — Link type not found:
Given: A LINK event where get_issue_link_types() returns types that do not include "Relates"
When: process_outbound() is called
Then: bridge_alert is written with enumerated available types, link creation is skipped

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py (Edit: add test class/functions for LINK event handling)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file contains at least 6 test functions covering LINK event handling
  Verify: grep -c "def test.*link" tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py | awk '{exit ($1 < 6)}'
- [ ] Tests fail when run (RED state — LINK handler does not exist yet)
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "link" --tb=short -q 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED\|ERROR"
- [ ] No existing tests broken
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "not link" --tb=short -q
